statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input format is 2 lines: The first line contains an integer n, representing the size of the queue. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the elements of the queue.
statement.outputFormat: Output a single integer representing the dequeued element.
statement.notes: This is a fault queue implementation. The queue will be initialized with the given size. The first element added to the queue is the one at the end of the array. Then, elements are dequeued from the end of the array as well.

examples.explanation: Initially, the queue is [1, 2, 3]. Dequeue from the end gives 2.

hints.text: The queue is implemented in reverse order. Think about how to dequeue an element from the end.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: The queue is implemented in reverse order. The problem description states that the first element added to the queue is the one at the end of the array.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach is to implement a standard queue using a list.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a circular buffer to store the elements of the queue.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The queue operations should follow the standard FIFO (First-In-First-Out) principle.
editorial.dryRun: For example, given a queue [1, 2, 3], the dequeue operation should return 1.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not following the standard queue implementation.
